Overview

This repository provides Simscape-based workflows for modeling, simulation, and stability assessment of renewable energy systems. It enables:

  • High IBR penetration and grid stability analysis
  • Workflows for Wind, HVDC, solar, battery energy storage systems (BESS) under conditions like faults, outages
  • Grid-forming (GFM) vs. grid-following (GFL) control comparison
  • Compliance evaluation with IEEE 2800, ENTSOE, AEMO, CEA, and UK grid codes
  • Fault ride-through (FRT), frequency/voltage support, and GFM control for HVDC
  • Admittance scanning for oscillation and stability detection

Get Started

Double-click on the RenewableEnergyDesignSimscape.prj file (root folder) to setup the project and get started.

Workflow

Navigate to different applications in the sections below. Click on the hyperlinks provided to go to the application workflows.

Renewable Energy Models

Battery Energy Storage with Grid Forming Control in PV Park

Validates how a GFM battery energy storage system improves grid strength and reliability with high IBR penetration in solar PV applications.

Open Model

Grid Forming Controls for Type 4 Wind Generators

Evaluates GFM converter control for type-4 wind turbines in maintaining stable operation with high IBR penetration in wind farms.

Open Model

Stability Analysis

Test Harness for Admittance Scan

Obtain frequency vs admittance characteristics with admittance scan block connected to an ideal grid for IBR characterization.

Open Model

Renewable Stability Assessment via Admittance Scan

Data-driven stability assessment to determine frequency vs admittance characteristics of grid-connected renewable systems.

Open Model

HVDC Stability Analysis with Offshore Wind

Two-terminal VSC-HVDC system stability assessment connecting offshore wind farms using admittance scan methodology.

Open Model

HVDC Systems for Offshore Wind Integration

Multi-Terminal HVDC for Offshore Wind Integration

Multi-terminal HVDC design for transporting power from multiple offshore wind farms with GFM control strategies.

Open Model

Black Start of Offshore Wind Farm via VSC-HVDC with GFM

Complete HVDC black-start sequence with grid-forming wind turbines: DC-link pre-charge, offshore P-control, onshore VSC grid-forming.

Open Model

Grid Code Compliance

Automated Grid Code Compliance Checker

Evaluate IBR plant performance against international grid standards (IEEE 2800, ENTSOE, AEMO, CEA, UK) with automated report generation including voltage/frequency limits, harmonics, LVRT/OVRT, and PQ/QV capability curves.

Open Model

Components

This project includes a custom Simulink library (renewableCustom_lib) with reusable blocks for renewable energy integration workflows. Open the library browser to explore all blocks:

open_system('renewableCustom_lib')

General

Scan Block
PRBS admittance scan instrument at the point of interconnection for IBR characterization.
Measurement (Three-Phase)
Inline three-phase measurement: voltage magnitude, frequency, active and reactive power.

HVDC

GFMHVDC Controller Onshore
Grid-forming controller for onshore HVDC station using DC-link inertia (VSM-based).
DC Voltage Controller
Regulates DC-link voltage and outputs modulation reference for VSC-HVDC systems.
HVDC Power Controller
Controls active power transfer through HVDC link with droop-based dispatch.
GFM Controller (Offshore)
Virtual synchronous machine for offshore AC voltage and frequency formation.
HVDC Station (Bipole)
Full bipolar HVDC station with MMC, transformer, and DC cables (400 kV, 400 MVA).

Renewable Sources

PV Controller
Grid-following PV controller with current limiting and LVRT support.
Inverter
Three-phase inverter with DC and AC terminals for PV and BESS applications.
GFM Wind (PMSG)
Grid-forming wind turbine with PMSG and inverter (12.5 MW, Type-4).
Wind GFL Mpp (PMSG)
Grid-following wind turbine with maximum power point tracking.
GFL Controller
Grid-following controller with PLL, droop, and current limiting.

Test

This project includes unit tests for all major applications in the Tests folder. Run all tests from the command line.

Requirements

This project requires MATLAB® R2025b or later, Simulink®, Simscape™, Simscape Electrical™, and Simulink Control Design™.
